articles.title: Sakana Fugu
articles.summary: Sakana Fugu is a multi-agent system that behaves like a single model. Fugu can decide whether to handle requests directly or coordinate a team of expert models. It manages model selection, delegation, verification, and synthesis. Users simply call one model, and a coordinated system of experts does the work. Sakana Fugu and Fugu Ultra are available today through a single OpenAI-compatible API.

articles.title: Inception Labs' Mercury 2 AI Beats Google's DiffusionGemma at Its Own Game
articles.summary: Mercury 2 is a reasoning language model that generates about 1,000 tokens per second. It uses diffusion, the same trick that turns static into a photo in image generators like Stable Diffusion. The model is best for speed-sensitive, high-volume parts of workflows rather than the hardest frontier reasoning. It is only available via API/cloud.

articles.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
articles.summary: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for Anthropic after nine years. Jumper, who co-led the AlphaFold team, won a Nobel Prize for predicting protein structures. His departure follows struggles at DeepMind in selling coding tools to businesses.

articles.title: AI Pauses
articles.summary: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 were shut down by the White House via an imposition of export controls. The Trump Administration said it was due to a jailbreak of Fable - this turned out to just be saying 'fix this code'. Anthropic has been told to fix this 'jailbreak', which is impossible. It's now been over a week since the pause in deployment and the situation has yet to improve.

articles.title: Auditing DiffusionGemma Transparency
articles.summary: A transparency audit found that DiffusionGemma remained similarly monitorable to Gemma despite its diffusion-based architecture. The analysis highlighted a gap between variable transparency and algorithmic transparency, and explored phenomena such as non-chronological reasoning, token smearing, and intermediate-context reasoning.

articles.title: Optimizing Models to Be Fast at Codegen
articles.summary: Morph LLM optimizes open coding models by training a drafter on coding output, not the internet, for faster speculative decoding, achieving a 3.07x speedup. Autoresearch automates kernel tuning for low-demand GPUs like NVIDIA and AMD, enhancing warp-decode kernels to reach 162 tok/s on affordable hardware. Interconnect over PCIe replaces expensive NVLink using custom kernels, maintaining performance by sharing caches via TCP, cutting time-to-first-token by 84%.

articles.title: From Prompting Agents to Loop Engineering
articles.summary: AI coding workflows are shifting from prompt engineering to loop engineering, where developers build systems that repeatedly prompt, evaluate, and re-prompt agents until a measurable goal is achieved.

articles.title: Nvidia's Autonomous Robotics Research
articles.summary: NVIDIA ENPIRE is a closed-loop framework that enables coding agents to iteratively improve real-world robot policies through automated resets, evaluation, verification, and refinement.

articles.title: A viral doomsday scenario aims to shake Europe out of its AI complacency
articles.summary: A speculative thought experiment called Europe 2031, penned by Brussels-based thinktankers, paints a picture of a world where Europe's lack of investment into datacenters results in it being far behind the US and China. The lack of investment results in Europe's economy being in shambles as it lacks its own AI. In this world, populism is surging, the Euro is wobbling, and cyber-attacks are shredding EU businesses.

articles.title: Notes on the Industry Job Search
articles.summary: A large part of the job search journey involves managing all of the emotions that come with being on the market. There's a lot of social perception to navigate. It can be stressful navigating a huge decision space with incomplete information where small choices have an outsized impact. This post takes a look at what the job search experience is like right now.

articles.title: There is minimal downside to switching to open models
articles.summary: New safeguards on recent models make the idea of running open models on private machines much more appealing.

articles.title: LLMs are complicated now
articles.summary: Attention might be all you need, but modern models use many different variants of it.

articles.title: AI Engineer Claims to Have Cracked Linear A
articles.summary: Linear A is a Bronze Age writing system that appeared sometime around 1800 BC and was used until 1450 BC.

articles.title: Solving an ARD problem in AI: Agentic Resource Discovery
articles.summary: Tech giants, including Google, Microsoft, and Cisco, are developing the Agentic Resource Discovery (ARD) protocol.

articles.title: When Ideology Becomes Systemic Risk
articles.summary: Companies led by highly ideological founders eventually trigger resistance from regulators, investors, customers, and governments as their influence grows beyond traditional institutional controls.
